title: Singularities in reductions of Shimura varieties
speaker: Thomas Haines
abstract: The singularities in the reduction modulo $p$ of the modular curve $Y_0(p)$ are visualized by the famous picture of two curves meeting transversally at the supersingular points. It is a fundamental question to understand the singularities which arise in the reductions modulo $p$ of integral models of Shimura varieties. For PEL type Shimura varieties with parahoric level structure at $p$, this question has been studied since the 1990's.

proof_stages.label: Singularity hierarchy and historical Siegel motivation
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes the hierarchy of singularities to be tested—regular, lci, Gorenstein, Cohen-Macaulay, normal, rational—and recalls the history of the Siegel Gamma_0(p) case as evidence that normal Cohen-Macaulay behavior is the expected optimal general result.

proof_stages.label: Shimura varieties with parahoric level and reduction to local models
proof_stages.summary: A Shimura datum, local reflex field, conjugacy class of cocharacters, and parahoric subgroup are fixed, and the key comparison theorem is invoked: suitable integral models of abelian-type Shimura varieties are étale-locally modeled by the corresponding Pappas-Zhu local model.

proof_stages.label: Pappas-Zhu local-model construction
proof_stages.summary: The local model is constructed by spreading the parahoric group scheme over a two-dimensional Beilinson-Drinfeld Grassmannian degeneration and taking the scheme-theoretic closure of the generic affine-Grassmannian Schubert variety, yielding a projective flat scheme over the reflex integers.

proof_stages.label: Admissible Schubert special fiber and main theorem
proof_stages.summary: The reduced special fiber is identified as the admissible locus, a finite union of affine Schubert varieties, and the main theorem states that under mild normality assumptions the actual geometric special fiber is reduced, the local model is normal, and for p>2 it is Cohen-Macaulay with explicitly described dualizing sheaf.

proof_stages.label: Normality hypotheses and Schubert-variety input
proof_stages.summary: The normality assumption is shown to hold in broad good-characteristic or minuscule situations but to be necessary in bad characteristic, and the proof will use the theorem that normalizations of affine Schubert varieties are Cohen-Macaulay, have rational singularities, and are Frobenius split in positive characteristic.

proof_stages.label: Reducedness from coherence-conjecture dimension comparison
proof_stages.summary: Zhu's coherence conjecture supplies a relatively ample line bundle whose global-section dimensions agree on the local-model special fiber and on the admissible locus, forcing the special fiber to equal its reduced admissible locus.

proof_stages.label: Normality and equal-characteristic reduction of Cohen-Macaulayness
proof_stages.summary: Using the Schubert-variety input, the generic fiber is normal and Cohen-Macaulay; together with reduced special fiber this gives normality of the whole local model by Serre's criterion, and Cohen-Macaulayness is reduced to the common combinatorial special fiber, hence to the equal-characteristic function-field local model.

proof_stages.label: Relative Frobenius splitting and the Cohen-Macaulay conclusion
proof_stages.summary: In equal characteristic the strengthened coherence argument gives Frobenius splitting relative to the special-fiber Cartier divisor, and the Blickle-Schwede lemma then upgrades Cohen-Macaulayness away from that divisor to Cohen-Macaulayness of the entire local model.

examples.name: Siegel modular variety with Gamma_0(p) level
examples.note: The Siegel moduli problem, including the low-genus special-fiber behavior, illustrates the historical evidence for normal/Cohen-Macaulay singularities and later serves as a concrete instance of the general parahoric local-model setup.
examples.engagement_reason: He describes the Siegel Gamma_0(p) moduli problem and states known singularity results for small and general genus, but does not compute or verify them in the talk.

passes.reason: a standard Siegel parahoric-level Shimura variety used as the motivating test case

examples.name: Zhu's non-Gorenstein affine Schubert variety
examples.note: This counterexample explains why the general singularity statement cannot usually be strengthened from Cohen-Macaulay to Gorenstein.
examples.engagement_reason: He invokes Zhu’s non-Gorenstein affine Schubert variety only as an existence example showing Gorensteinness fails in general.

examples.name: Bad-characteristic Schubert varieties for PGL_n, especially PGL_2 in characteristic 2
examples.note: These nonnormal Schubert varieties show that the normality hypothesis in the theorem is genuinely necessary in bad characteristic.
